items.title: VI Live: VAR draait rode kaart-beslissing terug bij Feyenoord - Telstar
items.summary: Feyenoord sluit zondag het Eredivisie-weekend af met een thuiswedstrijd tegen Telstar. De twee ploegen staan om 20.00 uur tegenover elkaar. Doet Feyenoord goede zaken in de strijd om plek twee? Je volgt het in deze VI Live. In onze andere VI Live kun je voor het overige voetbal terecht.

items.title: 'Heist' at Forest boosts Liverpool's top-four hopes
items.summary: Liverpool's late 1-0 win at Nottingham Forest puts the Reds firmly in the Champions League hunt after a "robbery" of a victory at the City Ground.

items.title: European recap: Arsenal enjoy impressive derby win at Spurs
AC Milan pijnlijk onderuit: achterstand op Inter nu tien punten
items.summary: Internazionale kan de champagne alvast koud gaan zetten. Achtervolger AC Milan leed zondag een pijnlijke nederlaag tegen Parma, waardoor de achterstand op koploper Inter is opgelopen tot tien punten.

items.title: Eze and Gyökeres at the double Arsenal boost title bid with Spurs rout
items.summary: Arsenal could feel the breath of Manchester City on their necks and the questions mounting; the anxiety all around them. The draw at Wolves on Wednesday had been a disaster and, with only two Premier League wins in seven, everybody seemed to want to say the same thing. Mikel Arteta and his players were cracking up in their pursuit of the title. This was the soothing tonic they craved, a comfortable and confident dismissal of a Tottenham team desperate to feel a new manager bounce under Igor Tudor. Spurs did show personality to find a Randal Kolo Muani goal for 1-1 in the 34th minute; it was the striker’s first for the club in the league. Continue reading...

items.title: Geïrriteerde Van Persie: 'Je moet kijken naar waarom ik een keuze wél maak'
items.summary: Raheem Sterling gaat tegen Telstar zijn eerste minuten maken voor Feyenoord, zo heeft Robin van Persie bevestigd. De trainer van Feyenoord heeft ook uitgelegd waarom hij voor Jordan Bos als linksbuiten kiest, al oogde hij wat geïrriteerd.

items.title: Eze helps Arsenal settle title nerves with NLD win
items.summary: Eberechi Eze produced a timely brace to haunt Tottenham again and restore Arsenal's five-point lead at the Premier League summit with a 4-1 north London derby win.

items.title: MLS: Messi did not break policy in alleged video
items.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating allegations that Lionel Messi gained unauthorized access to the officials' locker room following the match between Inter Miami and LAFC on Saturday at the LA Memorial Coliseum.

items.title: Pep recommends cocktails after 'massive' City win
items.summary: Pep Guardiola hailed a "massive" win after title-chasing Manchester City held on for a crucial yet nervy victory over Newcastle on Saturday.

items.title: Arteta: Players must accept 'noise' in title race
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has told his title-chasing players to join another club if they can't handle the "noise and bullets" that comes with fighting for the Premier League.

items.title: History maker: Ka Ying Rising notches 18th successive win
items.summary: Hong Kong superstar Ka Ying Rising extended his unbeaten run on Sunday morning, streaking clear to win the Queen’s Silver Jubilee Cup in track record time and never looked out of third gear.

items.title: VI Live: AC Milan bijt zich stuk op Parma, feest voor Inter lonkt
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op het programma. De wedstrijd van Feyenoord volg je in een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je in deze VI Live terecht, net als voor al het buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Eze wéér hoofdrolspeler tegen Spurs: Arsenal voorkomt nieuwe misstap in titelrace
items.summary: Arsenal heeft zondag geen nieuwe misstap begaan in de Engelse titelstrijd. In de North London Derby zegevierde de ploeg van Mikel Arteta tegen aartsrivaal Tottenham Hotspur, waar manager Igor Tudor debuteerde. Net als in het heenduel eerder dit seizoen, werd uitgerekend Eberechi Eze de man van de wedstrijd.

items.title: Michael Buckley has Melbourne Cup dream for Constitution Hill
items.summary: After burning up the Southwell straight on Friday, many are questioning where we will see Constitution Hill next with plans to run in the Champion Hurdle next month still in the balance but his owner has his sights on a big day out in November.

items.title: McIlroy begins attempt to chase down Bridgeman at Genesis Invitational LIVE!
Wakefield hold off Huddersfield fightback to claim narrow win
items.summary: Wakefield held off a late Huddersfield fightback as they picked up their first victory of the Betfred Super League season with an 18-16 away win at the Accu Stadium.

items.title: Scottish Premiership: Rangers and Celtic lose ground on Hearts amid late drama
items.summary: Rangers draw despite late comeback at Livingston Hibs’ Kai Andrews strikes in 87th minute to stun Celtic Celtic and Rangers both lost ground on Hearts in the Scottish Premiership title race, losing and drawing respectively amid dramatic scenes with late goals and dismissals on Sunday. Rangers fought back furiously from 2-0 down to draw 2-2 with 10-man Livingston in West Lothian but dropped two valuable points. Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea, Arsenal and Man City into Women's FA Cup quarters
items.summary: Holders Chelsea edged out Manchester United to reach the quarter-finals of the Women's FA Cup, while Arsenal and Man City cruise through with comfortable wins.

items.title: Women's FA Cup: Chelsea claim extra-time win as Arsenal, Man City progress
Girma winner sinks Manchester United to send Chelsea into Women’s FA Cup quarter-finals
items.summary: A 99th-minute winner from Naomi Girma sent holders Chelsea through to the Women’s FA Cup quarter-finals as they saw off a stubborn Manchester United 2-1. In a competitive encounter at Kingsmeadow, Sonia Bompastor’s side needed extra time to keep their Cup defence alive after Simi Awujo had cancelled out Sam Kerr’s opener in normal time. “I am really pleased because this competition is really special for us,” Bompastor said. “We want to go as far as possible. It was a tight game because Man United are a great team. I am really pleased with the result and the performance.” Continue reading...
